keywords.name: Ninjutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Ninjutsu keyword use your Ninjutsu attack bonus or Save DC when cast.

keywords.name: Genjutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Genjutsu keyword use your Genjutsu attack bonus or Save DC when cast. All Genjutsu have at least one Stimuli keyword (Visual, Auditory, Inhaled, Tactile, Unaware); to be immune to a Genjutsu with multiple Stimuli, you need immunity to all of them.

keywords.name: Taijutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Taijutsu keyword use your Taijutsu attack bonus or Save DC when cast.

keywords.name: Bukijutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Bukijutsu keyword use your Taijutsu attack bonus or Save DC when cast.

keywords.name: Fuinjutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Fuinjutsu keyword are summoning or sealing jutsu. Casting requires Chakra Seals.

keywords.name: Sensory
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Sensory keyword are designed to track and find other creatures through manipulation of chakra.

keywords.name: Clash
keywords.description:
Clashing Jutsu: While most jutsu will never collide with one another, there are some that will. When this collision happens a Jutsu Clash is initiated. Jutsu with the Clash keyword that are used in the same turn (or round, depending on the jutsu/ability) initiates a clash. When a clash is initiated the opposing users of either ability or jutsu must make opposing checks using certain skills depending on the jutsu used.

• Ninjutsu Clash: Intelligence (Ninshou) check vs an opposing Intelligence (Ninshou) check.
• Taijutsu Clash: Strength or Dexterity (Martial Arts) check vs an opposing Strength or Dexterity (Martial Arts) check.
• Ninjutsu vs Taijutsu Clash: Follow the above rules for the triggering jutsu.

When you clash jutsu of different ranks, the difference in power becomes painfully apparent. For every rank higher a jutsu is compared to the one it is clashing, the caster gains one rank of Mastery to the clash check. The winner of the Jutsu Clash applies their jutsu's effects while the loser's jutsu is dispelled and they gain 1 rank of Concussed and are Dazed.

Best of 3: Depending on the magnitude of jutsu being used, a Clash may not always be decided with a single roll. When two jutsu of B-Rank or higher are initiated in a Jutsu Clash, both parties shall instead make 3 Jutsu Clash contests against the other in a best of 3 (the clasher that wins two or more contests, wins the clash).

Elemental Advantage: When two different Ninjutsu of differing nature releases clash, you use the Elemental Advantage rules. Each of the 5 Nature Releases is strong or weak to another, with some being neutral. When this interaction happens, the user of the superior element makes any contested attacks or checks (such as Clash checks) at advantage, while the user of the inferior element rolls normally.

Circle of Elemental Superiority (left is superior to right):
Fire > Wind > Lightning > Earth > Water > Fire

keywords.name: Barrier
keywords.description: Conjures, creates, or summons a defensive structure or force field that protects the caster or a designated creature. Cast as a reaction, the effect always applies to the triggering damage instance — possibly turning a hit into a miss or reducing damage. Barrier structures with their own HP intercept damage; if reduced to 0, excess damage carries over to the protected creature.

keywords.name: Stable
keywords.description: Applies only to concentration jutsu with a duration greater than instant. The caster cannot lose concentration on this jutsu as a result of taking damage.

keywords.name: Efficient
keywords.description: Applies only to concentration jutsu with a duration greater than instant. The caster does not need to spend chakra to maintain concentration.

keywords.name: Interrupt
keywords.description: Reserved for jutsu with a Reaction casting time. The Interrupt jutsu's effects apply before the triggering effect occurs, with the express purpose of preventing it entirely. Unless otherwise specified, the action and resource cost of the interrupted action is still paid.

keywords.name: Combination
keywords.description: Multiple casters can cast the jutsu simultaneously. Cost is split evenly between participants, and the effect is magnified per the Combination text. Casters who know the jutsu may join as a reaction; casters who don't but have all required keyword access within 30 ft may make a Ninshou, Martial Arts, or Illusion check vs the Save DC to assist.

keywords.name: Clone
keywords.description: Reserved for Ninjutsu. Creates duplicates controlled by the summoner, resembling the summoner's appearance. Clones use the Clone stat block (Summon Stat Blocks section) with per-jutsu tweaks. Commanded by the summoner spending an Action or Bonus Action; on command, a clone moves up to its speed and may take a Clone Action. Clones share 1 Reaction per round collectively.

keywords.name: Visual
keywords.description: Tricks the target's visual perception. Creatures with Blindness are immune to jutsu carrying only this Stimuli keyword.

keywords.name: Auditory
keywords.description: Tricks the target's perception of sound. Deafness provides immunity to jutsu carrying only this Stimuli keyword.

keywords.name: Inhaled
keywords.description: Triggered by inhaling a poison or drug. Immunity to the Envenomed condition provides immunity to jutsu carrying only this Stimuli keyword.

keywords.name: Tactile
keywords.description: Tricks the target's perception of touch, allowing the Genjutsu to deal damage. Immunity to Psychic damage provides immunity to jutsu carrying only this Stimuli keyword.

keywords.name: Unaware
keywords.description: Bypasses a creature's ability to discern real from fake. Becoming aware of an Unaware Genjutsu requires a successful Identify Genjutsu skill action. Not a Stimuli.

keywords.name: Combo
keywords.description: Until the end of your turn, you can target one affected creature with a Taijutsu Finisher regardless of range, once per turn, using either an Action or Bonus Action — ignoring its listed casting time.

keywords.name: Finisher
keywords.description: You can only use a jutsu's Finisher effect if you cast a Taijutsu with the Combo keyword immediately prior in the same turn.

keywords.name: Chain
keywords.description:
Special Effect: Chain. Jutsu with this keyword are a dance of steel and are treated as steps. You must spend an Action casting a Jutsu with the Chain keyword to start a Chain. There are 3 levels of Chains: Chain 1, Chain 2, and Chain 3. When you spend your Action to cast a Bukijutsu with the Chain keyword, before the effects of your jutsu are applied you can either raise your Chain rank by +1, remain at your current Chain rank, or not benefit from chains at all.

If you cast a Bukijutsu as a Bonus action or Reaction with the Chain keyword, you can choose to remain at your current Chain rank for the round. If you have not raised or maintained your Chain rank before the start of your next turn, then at the beginning of your turn your Chain rank resets to 0.

When you have a Chain rank of 1 or higher, Bukijutsu you cast with the Chain keyword may have effects attached to them based on your current Chain rank. You can only use the effects associated with your Chain rank.

keywords.name: Hijutsu
keywords.description: Unique to Clans. Jutsu with this keyword can be learned and cast ignoring ability score requirements, Medical/elemental keyword limitations, and the Kinjutsu keyword. Branch-family unique keywords are still required even with Hijutsu.

keywords.name: Kinjutsu
keywords.description: Forbidden Techniques — barred from being taught across the Five Great Nations. Cover complex techniques causing grotesque/grave bodily injury, defilement, communion with the dead, or that are too powerful for general use. Learnable only by spending downtime with a master familiar with the desired jutsu, unless granted by a class or clan feature.

keywords.name: Medical
keywords.description: Considered Medical Ninjutsu. You must have a feature or feat that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list.

keywords.name: Earth Release
keywords.description:
Considered Earth Release jutsu — you must have a feature or feat that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list (such as the Nature Release feat). Earth Release is naturally strong against Water Release and weak against Lightning Release.

Special Effect: All structures and constructs made by a Ninjutsu with the Earth Release keyword have resistance to cold damage and vulnerability to lightning damage, even if the jutsu does not specifically state its resistances or immunities. Additionally, unless stated otherwise, structures summoned by Earth Release jutsu remain as structures after the jutsu's duration ends.

Unique Mechanic — Earthen Artifact: Unlike normal constructs which are often recognized as creatures, Earthen Artifacts are a unique type of Object, Structure, and Construct that is recognized as such only when an effect or feature references it. Objects and Structures treated as an Earthen Artifact do not gain or lose traits, hit points, or any other statistic, and instead can interact with any feature, jutsu, or effect that specifically mentions it in its referenced way.

keywords.name: Wind Release
keywords.description: Considered Wind Release jutsu — you must have a feature or feat that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list (such as the Nature Release feat). Wind Release is naturally strong against Lightning Release and weak against Fire Release.

keywords.name: Fire Release
keywords.description: Considered Fire Release jutsu — you must have a feature or feat that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list (such as the Nature Release feat). Fire Release is naturally strong against Wind Release and weak against Water Release.

keywords.name: Water Release
keywords.description:
Considered Water Release jutsu — you must have a feature or feat that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list (such as the Nature Release feat). Water Release is naturally strong against Fire Release and weak against Earth Release.

Sources of Water: Water Release jutsu are most easily performed using existing water sources, such as lakes or rivers. Users can also create water within their bodies with chakra, which they expel from their mouths, though this is generally regarded as a testament of skill. Water Release is typically used offensively — battering damage from sheer volume, or slashing/piercing pressure cuts — and can also trap targets with dense or sticky water, shield the user, or obscure vision with mist or invisibility-grade water droplets. Many Water Release jutsu have an alternate effect when cast near a sufficient source of water: typically reducing chakra cost by 2, or granting an enhanced effect such as additional range, extra targets, or condition removal as part of the cast.

keywords.name: Lightning Release
keywords.description:
Considered Lightning Release jutsu — you must have a feature or feat that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list (such as the Nature Release feat). Lightning Release is naturally strong against Earth Release and weak against Wind Release.

Overcharge: Some Lightning Release jutsu carry an Overcharge entry — a heightened effect that activates under conditions defined by the jutsu (typically when a target fails its saving throw, or when the caster opts into it). Overcharge varies per jutsu — examples on page 105 include adding an additional rank of Shaken on a failed save (Banquet of Lightning), or transforming a single-target attack into a 60-foot line that forces a Dexterity save and inflicts concentration disadvantage on creatures hit while concentrating on a jutsu (Guiding Bolt). Higher-rank casts may also expand or augment the Overcharge effect — see each jutsu's At Higher Ranks entry for specifics.

keywords.name: Unique
keywords.description: Jutsu, clan, and class features sometimes append keywords found nowhere else (such as the Hyūga clan's Branch keywords). Jutsu with these keywords can only be accessed or cast when a feature or effect explicitly grants the ability to do so.
